final_prompt.prompt:
Create an editorial photo of a red convertible driving on Route 66 through the desert at golden hour.

Style/medium: vintage American road-trip photography, Kodachrome film aesthetic, 1960s travel magazine editorial
Composition: wide-angle landscape shot, 24mm lens, car positioned on lower third following rule of thirds, leading road lines drawing eye to horizon
Subject: classic red convertible with chrome details, top down, two passengers silhouettes visible, dust trailing behind
Scene/backdrop: endless stretch of Route 66 cutting through arid desert, scattered saguaro cacti on both sides, distant mesas on horizon
Lighting/mood: golden hour sunset, warm side-lighting casting long shadows, sky gradients from deep orange to dusty rose, sense of freedom and wanderlust
Color palette: warm orange-red sunset sky as dominant, vibrant red car as focal anchor, muted sage desert greens, dusty beige earth tones
Materials/textures: visible film grain, slightly faded color saturation characteristic of vintage prints, chrome reflections, dust particles in air
Avoid: oversaturated neon colors, modern cars, pristine asphalt, 8K hyper-realistic rendering, lens flares unless natural
